purpose: Per-feature cross-document novelty PROFILE (GENIE axis, arXiv:2606.12790): for ONE target document against a supplied reference POOL, reports — per reused stdlib stylometry feature — the target's z-position relative to the pool's distribution of that feature, plus the per-feature mean/SD/n and a per-family |z| distribution summary. The per-feature distribution + per-feature position table ARE the read — NEVER a single "novelty score", NEVER a verdict, NEVER a band. Clean-room of GENIE's per-feature-vs-population position idea, using the fixed stdlib feature families (function_words / punctuation / paragraph_dialogue / pronoun_modal_negation / char_ngrams_3/4/5) from extract_features(include_spacy=False) as the named feature set. M1 is model-free (mean/SD z, stdlib, no torch/spaCy), deterministic, and CI-runnable. The FEATURE-WISE COMPLEMENT to the cluster-wise distinct_diversity_audit (that one partitions a SET into near-dup equivalence classes; this one asks, for ONE target vs a pool, which named stylometric features make it atypical). Neither subsumes the other — they are orthogonal reads. Robust median/MAD z is an explicitly DEFERRED M2 seam. NOT an AI/human call; NOT a plagiarism/derivative determination; NOT a selection/ranking signal; NO absolute band; thresholds are operator-side / PROVISIONAL.
use_when: You have ONE target document and a prompt-matched reference POOL (>= min_pool, default 5, usable docs of >= length_floor_words, default 100) and want to know WHICH named stylometric features make the target atypical relative to the pool's distribution
You want a per-feature read (which features differ, by how much) that a cluster partition (distinct_diversity_audit) or a global pairwise-cosine spread (homogeneity_audit) cannot give
You need a model-free, CI-runnable M1 read with no torch/spaCy dependency
do_not_use_when: You want an AI/human/authorship/derivative determination — a per-feature z-position is a measurement relative to THIS pool, not a provenance call; low novelty is NOT AI, high novelty is NOT human
The pool is single-source or mixed-prompt (inflates or deflates apparent atypicality); a prompt-matched pool is required
Target or pool is below the length floors (target < 100 words, or usable pool < 5 docs after length-floor filtering)
You want GENIE's learned task-feature figures — this M1 stdlib stylometric lens is NOT comparable to GENIE's learned task-specific features (arXiv:2606.12790)

inputs.pool: a --reference-manifest (JSONL; id + text|text_path) OR a --reference-dir of texts (.txt/.md, recursive)
inputs.knobs: --length-floor-words (default 100), --min-pool (default 5), --lens stylometric (M1) | embedding (POC-gated M2 seam, fails loud)

compute.cost_note: M1 pure-stdlib stylometric lens (extract_features(include_spacy=False) — 7 families: function_words / punctuation / paragraph_dialogue / pronoun_modal_negation / char_ngrams_3/4/5); no models, no numpy beyond arithmetic, no API, deterministic. The embedding M2 lens is a POC-gated seam (lazy-import + fail-loud missing_dependency), not in this build.

references: GENIE: Generative Note Information Extraction (arXiv:2606.12790): https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.12790
NoveltyBench: Evaluating Language Models for Humanlike Diversity (arXiv:2504.05228): https://arxiv.org/abs/2504.05228
